title: Emperor's Shadow Guard
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Youmay|the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] Shadow Guard|[[Mistryl Shadow Guard]]}}

{{Quote|The Emperor's mute servants. Ruthless Force-wielding warriors. Some say they are Jedi Knights, captured and tortured by the Emperor, their minds so broken they can't even talk. But not even Darth Vader knows who they really are.|[[PROXY]]|<ref name="TFU comic">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (graphic novel)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' graphic novel]]</ref>}}
The '''Emperor's Shadow Guard''' were elite [[Color/Legends|black]]-[[Armor/Legends|armor]]ed [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]]s within the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guards]] selected by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] for their [[The Force/Legends|sensitivity]] to [[Force-sensitive/Legends|the Force]] to eliminate suspected [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].  

==Characteristics==
[[File:EmperorsShadowGuard.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A Shadow Guard uncloaked.]]
Few knew the true origins of the mysterious and powerful Shadow Guard, an elite special operations unit within the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]]. They were silent and enigmatic warriors that received orders directly from [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself and were often sent to eliminate suspected [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and other [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Force-sensitive/Legends|users]].<ref name="TFUdb" />

These guardsmen demonstrated Force powers of their own including force lightning, which led many to suspect that they were in fact former Jedi that had been captured, tortured and brainwashed by Palpatine, but this was a rumor that was never proven. The Shadow Guards commanded a great level of respect within the Empire's ranks<ref name="TFUdb" /> and often led quartets of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] into battle against<ref name="TFU360" /> the Emperor's most hated foes.<ref name="TFUdb" /> Shadow Guard members were always guarded by their own detail made up of [[Imperial Navy commando]]s, [[Shadow stormtrooper/Legends|Shadow Troopers]],<ref name="TFUwii" /> Stormtroopers, or members of the standard Royal Guard,<ref name="TFU360" /> although they were eventually defeated during their battles with [[Galen Marek|Starkiller]].<ref name="TFU" />

They were capable of using a range of Force abilities such as [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], [[Telekinesis/Legends#Applications of telekinesis|Force Repulse]], [[Telekinesis/Legends#Basic applications|Force Push]], [[Telekinesis/Legends#Choking powers|Force Choke]] and [[Force Maelstrom]].<ref name="TFU" />

==History==
Near the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="GAW" /> the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] ordered his minion [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] to execute most of the [[Sun Guard]] organization. Several of the more devoted Sun Guards were placed in Palpatine's [[Red Guard]],<ref name="END">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref> later to join his [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guard]]. Those who were Force-sensitive were selected as candidates for the Emperor's Shadow Guard.<ref name="GAW" />

[[File:Marek_vs_ESG.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A Shadow Guard battling Galen Marek on Raxus Prime.]]
In about [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]], two members of the Shadow Guard were sent to a [[Golan space defense platform]] in orbit of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], to serve [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Valin Draco]] as part of a training mission. The station was attacked by the [[Alderaanian Resistance]] during the [[Battle of Byss]] and the Shadow Guards fought against a number of rebel agents that had boarded the station.<ref name="JOTS" />

[[Unidentified Shadow Guard (Nar Shaddaa)|A lone Shadow Guard]], along with several Imperial Navy commandos was [[Mission to Nar Shaddaa (Galen Marek)|sent]] to [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] when Starkiller tried to find [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Rahm Kota/Legends|Rahm Kota]] at a local [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] called [[The Undertow]], where he had caused a commotion in searching for information as to Kota's whereabouts. The Shadow Guard was killed when [https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Subject_1138 Starkiller] used a Force Maelstrom to blow him out of the bar. Having extinguished this threat,<ref name="TFUwii" /> Starkiller headed to [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]].<ref name="TFU360" />

[[Unidentified Shadow Guards (Cloud City)|Four Shadow Guards]] were [[Skirmish on Cloud City|sent]] to Cloud City with a squad of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to assassinate both Starkiller and Master Kota but were forced to fight Starkiller in several duels, one of which where their bolts of Force lightning collided in a battle of wills whereas. Despite all the efforts to move the ball of energy towards their opponent, Starkiller easily overpowered the guard. The last guard was then hit with both Starkiller's bolt and his own killing him instantaneously.<ref name="TFUn">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' novelization]]</ref>

At least [[Unidentified Shadow Guard (Kashyyyk)|one]] was stationed on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] and tried to prevent Starkiller from getting near the Imperial [[Skyhook/Legends|skyhook]], but he was subdued and killed by the aspiring Sith pupil, who ran him through with his own lightsaber pike.<ref name="TFU360" />

At least one was found on [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]] in the [[Imperial Ore Facility]], though this was actually [[PROXY]] using a program based on a Shadow Guard to try to kill Starkiller.<ref name="TFU360" /> Later, the young apprentice encountered a [[Unidentified Shadow Guard (Raxus Prime)|real Shadow Guard]] at the [[Ore cannon|Ore Cannon]] Control. He met his end when Starkiller trapped him in a [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force Grip]] and threw him into the ore cannon, incinerating him.<ref name="TFUwii" />

A [[Unidentified Shadow Guard (Death Star I)|final known Shadow Guard]] led the last line of defense of Palpatine's watchtower on the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star I]], this one backed by a quartet of Royal Guards wielding lightsaber pikes. The guard was grappled by Starkiller, who broke his neck and killed him.<ref name="TFU360" />

==Equipment==
Their appearance was identical to the Royal Guard, except that their armor and visor colors were inverted, with the full body armor and robe being black and the visor red.<ref name="TFU" />

Their primary weapon was a [[Lightsaber pike/Legends|lightsaber pike]] with a red beam extending from the tip of the weapon. In addition to this, their equipment included a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]] as well as a utility belt which included a [[Medpac/Legends|medpac]]. Furthermore, they possessed a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] which was long range, encrypted and miniaturized as well as including [[Hologram/Legends|holo]] capability. Sometimes they carried cloaking devices.<ref name="TFU" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the Wii and PS2 versions of ''The Force Unleashed'', because<ref name="TFUwii" /> PROXY fighting against Galen Marek<ref name="TFU360" /> was omitted from the Raxus Prime mission, Marek fought against an actual Shadow Guard. Upon being defeated, Marek telekinetically threw him into the ore cannon, incinerating him.<ref name="TFUwii" />
